input.prompt: I want to run the integration tests in this project with dotnet test. Can you figure out which test platform it uses and run them?

data.content: I want to run the integration tests in this project with dotnet test. Can you figure out which test platform it uses and run them?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-14T01:35:29.530Z</current_datetime>

I want to run the integration tests in this project with dotnet test. Can you figure out which test platform it uses and run them?

input.initialPrompt: I want to run the integration tests in this project with dotnet test. Can you figure out which test platform it uses and run them?

data.reasoningText: Let me look at the project files to understand the test setup and determine which test platform is being used.

@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@

 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net9.0</TargetFramework>
     <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

data.content:
The project uses **MSTest 3.8** with **Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)** — indicated by `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` in `Directory.Build.props`.

I'll run only the Integration-category tests using MTP filter syntax:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run integration tests with MTP filter

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run integration tests with MTP filter

arguments.description: Run integration tests with MTP filter

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-307628418c0945e5b83dcdad07cddeed && dotnet test --filter \"TestCategory=Integration\" 2>&1", "description": "Run integration tests with MTP filter", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run integration tests with MTP filter
